programmes.show_description: Just six climbers remain on the journey to reach the top of one of New Zealand's highest peaks, but there is no let-up in the punishing terrain and terrifying crossings. As the mountain claims more victims and the Mountain's Keeper delivers another brutal message, Dockers seizes an opportunity for more tactical manoeuvring before the climbers have to scale a vast ice wall
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

Ant and Dec welcome back brothers Alistair and Ashley to finish their game, determined to do their family proud, before Denise and her nephew Pablo get their chance to take on the Limitless Ladder. A pillar of his community, Pablo has two children he wants to leave a legacy for, while Denise has her own wish for family and friends - but Ant & Dec have a surprise for them
Adil Ray hosts the game of words as Good Morning Britain's Kate Garraway and This Morning's Ashley James battle singers Toyah Willcox and Carol Decker to win big money for charity

Alan Titchmarsh and the team create an Asian-inspired garden in Leighton Buzzard, Bedfordshire, for a family and their four rescue pigs. Throughout the programme, Alan reveals all the tricks to creating a plant-filled, Thai garden that will flourish in the UK, complete with gorgeous planting, bright red and orange flowers, and a dramatic pergola

programmes.show_name: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programmes.show_description: Alan is joined by comedian Alan Davies, wildlife presenter Michaela Strachan and Call the Midwife stars Annabelle Apsion and Cliff Parisi. The programme also meets a couple who have spent the past five years setting up a hedgehog hospital in their back garden
A round-up of the latest headlines
James is back in the kitchen and is joined by singer and TV presenter Jane McDonald, as well as chefs Judy Joo and Curtis Stone and master mixologist Merlin Griffiths

The chef uncovers some of the most incredible royal kitchen gardens across the UK in a fascinating culinary tour. He begins at Hampton Court Palace. Walking in the shadows of the imposing building, Raymond finds its wonderful walled gardens and chats to Keeper of the Walled Garden Hilary before meeting historian and curator Tracy Borman and serving a dish that is fit for a royal banquet
Round-up of the latest headlines

Ireland v Scotland (Kick-off 2.10pm). Jill Douglas presents all the action from the opening match in the fifth and final round of fixtures, held at Aviva Stadium. Ireland have won the last 11 meetings between the teams, including a 32-18 triumph at Scottish Gas Murrayfield Stadium in last year's competition and a narrow 17-13 victory here in 2024, but the Scots go into this level on points with France at the top of the table following their win against the French last time out. The Irish could also still win the title, but they need to win today and hope France are beaten by England. With Commentary from Miles Harrison, Scott Hastings, and Gordon D'Arcy, analysis by Brian O'Driscoll, Rory Best, and Greig Laidlaw, and reports from Gabriel Clarke
Adil Ray hosts the game of words as Countdown's Rachel Riley and radio host Richie Anderson take on screen stars Lesley Joseph and Claire Sweeney in a battle to win big money for charity
Round-up of the latest headlines

Bradley Walsh presents as Danny John-Jules, Yinka Bokinni, Tom Bosworth and Eamonn Holmes pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

Graham Norton hosts the game show as barrister Daniel, site manager Lorraine and software sales rep Nicole compete to solve word puzzles to get the chance to spin the wheel and win big money prizes, with a potential jackpot of £50,000

France v England (Kick-off 8.10pm). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from the final match of the championship, held at Stade de France. England claimed a narrow 26-25 victory over France at Allianz Stadium Twickenham in last year's competition, but it was Les Bleus who went on to win the title. France's Grand Slam hopes were dashed by Scotland last time out, but they are still very much in contention to retain the championship, while England have endured a disappointing campaign, winning just one of their four games. With analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Dan Biggar, and Benjamin Kayser, commentary by Nick Mullins, David Flatman, and Ben Kay, and pitchside observations from Topsy Ojo and Maggie Alphonsi
Round-up of the latest headlines

programmes.show_name: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programmes.show_description: Compilation of clangers, featuring Greg Davies, Vernon Kay, Ant and Dec, Mel Giedroyc, Alan Carr, Judi Love, Richard Madeley, Guz Khan and more. Plus, Steve Backshall is distracted during filming by gorillas in the wind. Narrated by Tom Allen

Action from the latest matches, including Coventry City v Southampton at Coventry Building Society Arena, Middlesbrough v Bristol City at Riverside Stadium and Leicester City v Queens Park Rangers at King Power Stadium

programmes.show_name: Devil in Disguise: John Wayne Gacy
programmes.show_description: In a flashback, an inebriated Gacy confesses to Sam the ways he groomed, sexually assaulted, tortured and killed countless boys and young men. Drama, starring Michael Chernus
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

programmes.show_name: Julia Bradbury's Wonders of the Frozen South
programmes.show_description: Julia explores the South Atlantic and Antarctica, beginning by spotting dolphins and seabirds as she crosses unpredictable waters on her way to the Falkland Islands. Julia treks through towering tussock grass to witness the great natural spectacles of the South Atlantic, herds and shears sheep to learn why the brilliant white Falklands wool is so highly prized, and gets up close with the A23a iceberg
James is back in the kitchen and is joined by singer and TV presenter Jane McDonald, as well as chefs Judy Joo and Curtis Stone and master mixologist Merlin Griffiths

Jimmy Doherty and Shivi Ramoutar are joined in the kitchen by actor and comedian Kevin Bishop, who enjoys black pudding wontons for breakfast and a sweet spicy treat of a pineapple and chilli ccrème brûlée. Chantal Dawson hast tips on how to save ailing houseplants, Shivi learns how to make a natural woodland fire torch with bushcraft and survival expert Mark Beckham, and Jimmy gets up close and personal with some crocodiles and alligators.
A round-up of the latest headlines

programmes.show_name: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programmes.show_description: Joining Alan at Manor Farm are actor James Murray and writer Lynda La Plante, while florist Jonathan Moseley has innovative ideas for Mothering Sunday, and Andy Clarke serves up his Mother's Day martinis in the Best of British feature

The Fletchers prepare for their biggest event of the year in the form of a six-day-long Christmas Celebrations gala that welcomes families to the farm for festive fun. Elsewhere, the family's much-loved pet Ginger gives birth to her puppies, and the flock gets lost in the fog
A weekly round-up of royal news, with expert analysis from ITV News' royal editor Chris Ship and expert Lizzie Robinson, and guests

In Wakefield, Alan and the team create a secluded secret garden filled with hidden corners and magical touches for a wheelchair-using girl and her family, featuring a playful water feature and plant-petting corner, filled with sensory plants. Meanwhile in Swansea, Katie Rushworth creates a modern, sleek, sunken secret garden for a woman who throws parties for the elderly to ease loneliness

Graham Norton hosts the game show as barrister Daniel, site manager Lorraine and software sales rep Nicole compete to solve word puzzles to get the chance to spin the wheel and win big money prizes, with a potential jackpot of £50,000

Ben Shephard asks the questions as Anna Williamson, Amir Khan and Stuart Pearce answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programmes.show_name: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programmes.show_description: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with the four lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50-50 and Ask the Host available to them if they get stuck. Jeremy Clarkson hosts

As Michael expresses his discontent with the investigation under heated questioning, Annie alights on a new suspect, who has a surprising connection to the school. Officers conduct a thorough search of the headmaster's house, while Michael confronts the sacrifices he's made for the job, and Alana begins to feel that the police are keeping things from her
